title: [SROA] Use shufflevector instead of select for vector blend
author: bjope
description:
A patch from May 2013, commit 1e211913b56f390, changed SROA into using a select instruction to perform vector blend. Idea was that using a select was the canonical form, and that we optimize select better than shufflevector.

This patch is changing SROA back into using shufflevector instead of select when doing the blend (inserting a smaller vector into a larger vector).

Motivation:
Nowadays InstCombine is canonicalizing this kind of vector blends, using vector select instructions, into a shufflevector instruction. So it is assumed that shufflevector is the canonical form now. It is also assumed that we are better at optimizing shufflevector today, compared to back in 2013.

Commit f26710d97d9c272be8a55 includes links to a discussion from 2016 (https://discourse.llvm.org/t/ir-canonicalization-vector-select-or-shufflevector/42257/6) about picking shufflevector as the canonical form.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

; Function Attrs: vscale_range(1,1)
define <vscale x 4 x i32> @cast_alloca_to_svint32_t(<vscale x 4 x i32> %type.coerce) #0 {
  %type = alloca <16 x i32>, align 64
  %type.addr = alloca <16 x i32>, align 64
  store <vscale x 4 x i32> %type.coerce, ptr %type, align 16
  %type1 = load <16 x i32>, ptr %type, align 64
  store <16 x i32> %type1, ptr %type.addr, align 64
  %1 = load <16 x i32>, ptr %type.addr, align 64
  %2 = load <vscale x 4 x i32>, ptr %type.addr, align 16
  ret <vscale x 4 x i32> %2
}

attributes #0 = { vscale_range(1,1) }

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-p1:16:16:16-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-f80:128-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

%S.vec3float = type { float, float, float }

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr noalias nocapture writeonly, ptr noalias nocapture readonly, i32, i1 immarg) #0

declare i32 @memcpy_vec3float_helper(ptr)

define i32 @memcpy_vec3float_widening(ptr %x) !prof !0 {
entry:
  %tmp1 = alloca %S.vec3float, align 4
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr align 4 %tmp1, ptr align 4 %x, i32 12, i1 false)
  %unused3 = load <4 x float>, ptr %tmp1, align 1
  %tmp2 = alloca %S.vec3float, align 4
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr align 4 %tmp2, ptr align 4 %tmp1, i32 12, i1 false)
  %result = call i32 @memcpy_vec3float_helper(ptr %tmp2)
  ret i32 %result
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

!0 = !{!"function_entry_count", i32 10}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define <4 x i64> @vector_ptrtoint({ <2 x ptr>, <2 x ptr> } %x) {
  %a = alloca { <2 x ptr>, <2 x ptr> }, align 32
  store { <2 x ptr>, <2 x ptr> } %x, ptr %a, align 16
  %vec = load <4 x i64>, ptr %a, align 32
  ret <4 x i64> %vec
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define <4 x ptr> @vector_inttoptr({ <2 x i64>, <2 x i64> } %x) {
  %a = alloca { <2 x i64>, <2 x i64> }, align 32
  store { <2 x i64>, <2 x i64> } %x, ptr %a, align 16
  %vec = load <4 x ptr>, ptr %a, align 32
  ret <4 x ptr> %vec
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define <4 x i32> @test_subvec_store() {
entry:
  %a = alloca <4 x i32>, align 16
  store <2 x i32> zeroinitializer, ptr %a, align 8
  %a.gep1 = getelementptr <4 x i32>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 1
  store <2 x i32> splat (i32 1), ptr %a.gep1, align 8
  %a.gep2 = getelementptr <4 x i32>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 2
  store <2 x i32> splat (i32 2), ptr %a.gep2, align 8
  %a.gep3 = getelementptr <4 x i32>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 3
  store i32 3, ptr %a.gep3, align 4
  %ret = load <4 x i32>, ptr %a, align 16
  ret <4 x i32> %ret
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write)
declare void @llvm.memset.p0.i32(ptr nocapture writeonly, i8, i32, i1 immarg) #0

define <4 x float> @test_subvec_memset() {
entry:
  %a = alloca <4 x float>, align 16
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i32(ptr %a, i8 0, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep1 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 1
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep1, i8 1, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep2 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 2
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep2, i8 3, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep3 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 3
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep3, i8 7, i32 4, i1 false)
  %ret = load <4 x float>, ptr %a, align 16
  ret <4 x float> %ret
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write) }

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define <4 x i32> @ptrLoadStoreTys(ptr %init, i32 %val2) {
  %val0 = load ptr, ptr %init, align 8
  %obj = alloca <4 x i32>, align 16
  store <4 x i32> zeroinitializer, ptr %obj, align 16
  store ptr %val0, ptr %obj, align 8
  %ptr2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %obj, i64 8
  store i32 %val2, ptr %ptr2, align 4
  %ptr3 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %obj, i64 12
  store i32 131072, ptr %ptr3, align 4
  %sroaval = load <4 x i32>, ptr %obj, align 16
  ret <4 x i32> %sroaval
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define i64 @test6(<4 x i64> %x, <4 x i64> %y, i64 %n) {
  %tmp = alloca { <4 x i64>, <4 x i64> }, align 32
  %p0 = getelementptr inbounds { <4 x i64>, <4 x i64> }, ptr %tmp, i32 0, i32 0
  store <4 x i64> %x, ptr %p0, align 32
  %p1 = getelementptr inbounds { <4 x i64>, <4 x i64> }, ptr %tmp, i32 0, i32 1
  store <4 x i64> %y, ptr %p1, align 32
  %addr = getelementptr inbounds { <4 x i64>, <4 x i64> }, ptr %tmp, i32 0, i32 0, i64 %n
  %res = load i64, ptr %addr, align 4
  ret i64 %res
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

define <4 x i32> @ptrLoadStoreTysAS3(ptr %init, i32 %val2) {
  %val0 = load ptr addrspace(3), ptr %init, align 8
  %obj = alloca <4 x i32>, align 16
  store <4 x i32> zeroinitializer, ptr %obj, align 16
  store ptr addrspace(3) %val0, ptr %obj, align 8
  %ptr2 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %obj, i64 8
  store i32 %val2, ptr %ptr2, align 4
  %ptr3 = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %obj, i64 12
  store i32 131072, ptr %ptr3, align 4
  %sroaval = load <4 x i32>, ptr %obj, align 16
  ret <4 x i32> %sroaval
}

target datalayout = "e-p:64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128-a0:0:64-n8:16:32:64"

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite)
declare void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr noalias nocapture writeonly, ptr noalias nocapture readonly, i32, i1 immarg) #0

define <4 x float> @test_subvec_memcpy(ptr %x, ptr %y, ptr %z, ptr %f, ptr %out) {
entry:
  %a = alloca <4 x float>, align 16
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr %a, ptr %x, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep1 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 1
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep1, ptr %y, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep2 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 2
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep2, ptr %z, i32 8, i1 false)
  %a.gep3 = getelementptr <4 x float>, ptr %a, i32 0, i32 3
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr %a.gep3, ptr %f, i32 4, i1 false)
  call void @llvm.memcpy.p0.p0.i32(ptr %out, ptr %a.gep2, i32 8, i1 false)
  %ret = load <4 x float>, ptr %a, align 16
  ret <4 x float> %ret
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: readwrite) }

comments.author: bjope
comments.body:
Stumbled upon this when looking at some downstream test diffs after https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/175199

With the vector select in the input (not running instcombine after sroa) we now get a freeze in the selection DAG when simplifying the select. And then we ended up with worse code compared to when using shufflevector. This fixes the problem at the root (not picking the vector select in the first place).
